question_text: A student has a mass of 50 kg. On Earth, gravitational field strength is 10 N/kg. On Mars, gravitational field strength is 3.7 N/kg. Calculate the difference in the student's weight between Earth and Mars.
explanation: First calculate weight on Earth: W = mg = 50 × 10 = 500 N. Then weight on Mars: W = 50 × 3.7 = 185 N. The difference is 500 - 185 = 315 N. This shows how much heavier you feel on Earth compared to Mars.
